content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA, consisting of NNN integers.

Consider the following definitions:


Prefix xor array of an array AAA is defined as the array BBB such that Bi=A1‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēAiB_i = A_1 \oplus \ldots \oplus A_iBi‚Äč=A1‚Äč‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēAi‚Äč, where ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes bitwise XOR operation.

In other words, B=[A1,A1‚äēA2,‚Ä¶,A1‚äēA2‚Ä¶‚äēAN]B=[A_1, A_1 \oplus A_2, \ldots, A_1 \oplus A_2 \ldots \oplus A_N]B=[A1‚Äč,A1‚Äč‚äēA2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,A1‚Äč‚äēA2‚Äč‚Ä¶‚äēAN‚Äč]
The value of an array AAA is the number of distinct values in array BBB. For example, for array A=[1,2,3,0]A=[1, 2, 3, 0]A=[1,2,3,0], we have B=[1,3,0,0]B=[1, 3, 0, 0]B=[1,3,0,0]. The array BBB has 333 distinct values, thus, the value of array AAA is 333.
One right shift on the array AAA is a transformation that changes the array A=[A1,A2‚Ä¶,AN]A=[A_1, A_2 \ldots, A_N]A=[A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč] to A‚Ä≤=[AN,A1,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1]A^{'}=[A_N, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}]A‚Ä≤=[AN‚Äč,A1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1‚Äč].
Calculate the **maximum** value of the array AAA you can get, by performing any (possibly zero) number of right shifts on the array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input contains TTT ‚ąí-‚ąí the number of test cases you need to solve.
The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN ‚ąí-‚ąí the size of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ąí-‚ąí the elements of the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of an array AAA you can achieve after performing any (possibly zero) number of right shifts on the array.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
**Subtask 1 (20 points):** Sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Perform zero right shifts on A=[0,0]A=[0,0]A=[0,0]. Thus, B=[0,0‚äē0]=[0,0]B= [0, 0\oplus0]=[0,0]B=[0,0‚äē0]=[0,0]. Thus, the value of array AAA is 111.
Test case 222: Perform two right shifts on A=[1,1,1,2,2,2]A = [1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2]A=[1,1,1,2,2,2]. Thus, AAA becomes [2,2,1,1,1,2][2, 2, 1, 1, 1, 2][2,2,1,1,1,2]. The array B=[2,0,1,0,1,3]B = [2, 0, 1, 0, 1, 3]B=[2,0,1,0,1,3]. Thus, the value of AAA is 444. It can be shown that the value of AAA cannot exceed 444.
Test case 333: Perform three right shifts on A=[1,2,2,8]A = [1, 2, 2, 8]A=[1,2,2,8]. Thus, AAA becomes [2,2,8,1][2, 2, 8, 1][2,2,8,1]. The array B=[2,2‚äē2,2‚äē2‚äē8,2‚äē2‚äē8‚äē1]=[2,0,8,9]B = [2, 2\oplus 2, 2\oplus 2\oplus 8, 2\oplus 2\oplus 8\oplus 1] = [2, 0, 8, 9]B=[2,2‚äē2,2‚äē2‚äē8,2‚äē2‚äē8‚äē1]=[2,0,8,9]. Thus, the value of AAA is 444. It can be shown that the value of AAA cannot exceed 444.
